title: compat: Add Health field to /containers/json endpoint
body:
#### Checklist

- [x] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all commits. (`git commit -s`).
- [x] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #27786` in commit message (if applicable)
- [x] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [x] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [ ] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [x] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
Add Health field to /containers/json endpoint, providing container health status in the summary output.
```

Fixes: #27786

comments.body:
@jankaluza I think I have satisfied all requests. 

I'm ready for a new review!
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.

This bot never works the first time!

I'll start a new trigger!

LGTM.

This looks good to me now, but it needs review from one of the maintainers :-).

I added a new small test that ensures that if a container is shut down, the API does not say that it is “healthy” or “unhealthy,” but correctly says that there is no active health status; therefore none
@mheon PTAL, thanks!
Why is this being added to the Docker-compat endpoint? I don't see if in the Docker API documentation.

@Honny1 this PR fixes a specific user-facing bug.

The change is small and self-contained, so I think it's worth merging now rather than blocking on the broader moby/moby migration.
CC: @jankaluza
Alright, so it's in 1.52... We really need to get up to speed on API changes.
Can we restrict this to only send if API version 1.52 or above is requested?
@mheon I think I did it, can you check if I did it correctly?
